66	class TreeNode:
67	
68	    counter = 0
69	    last_access_time_counter_float = float64(1.0)
70	
71	    def __init__(self, id: Optional[int] = None):
72	        self.children = defaultdict(TreeNode)
73	        self.parent: TreeNode = None
74	        self.key: RadixKey = None
75	        self.value: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
76	        self.mamba_value: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
77	        self.mamba_host_value: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
78	        # invariant: for any node, if mamba_lock_ref is locked, full_lock_ref must be locked;
79	        # if full_lock_ref is locked, mamba_lock_ref doesn't need to be locked. So,
80	        # full_lock_ref is always >= mamba_lock_ref.
81	        # for full_lock, once it is locked, its parent must be locked as well
82	        # for mamba_lock, it only need lock node itself
83	        self.full_lock_ref = 0
84	        self.mamba_lock_ref = 0
85	        # last access time is only used for sanity check. LRU is maintained by the lru list.
86	        self.last_access_time = get_last_access_time()
87	
88	        self.hit_count = 0
89	        self.host_ref_counter = 0
90	        self.host_mamba_ref_counter = 0
91	        # store the host indices of KV cache
92	        self.host_value = None
93	        # store hash values of each pages
94	        self.hash_value: Optional[List[str]] = None
95	
96	        # for lru list, invariant:
97	        # 1. prev has greater last_access_time
98	        # 2. next has smaller last_access_time
99	        self.prev = None
100	        self.next = None
101	        self.mamba_prev = None
102	        self.mamba_next = None
103	        self.host_mamba_prev = None
104	        self.host_mamba_next = None
105	
106	        self.id = TreeNode.counter if id is None else id
107	        TreeNode.counter += 1
108	
109	    @property
110	    def evicted(self):
111	        return self.value is None
112	
113	    @property
114	    def mamba_evicted(self):
115	        return self.mamba_value is None
116	
117	    @property
118	    def backuped(self):
119	        return self.host_value is not None
120	
121	    @property
122	    def mamba_backuped(self):
123	        return self.mamba_host_value is not None
124	
125	    def protect_host(self):
126	        """Protect the host KV value from eviction."""
127	        self.host_ref_counter += 1
128	
129	    def release_host(self):
130	        """Release the host KV value, allowing it to be evicted."""
131	        if self.host_ref_counter > 0:
132	            self.host_ref_counter -= 1
133	        else:
134	            raise RuntimeError("Host reference counter is already zero.")
135	
136	    def protect_host_mamba(self):
137	        """Protect the host mamba value from eviction."""
138	        self.host_mamba_ref_counter += 1
139	
140	    def release_host_mamba(self):
141	        """Release the host mamba value, allowing it to be evicted."""
142	        if self.host_mamba_ref_counter > 0:
143	            self.host_mamba_ref_counter -= 1
144	        else:
145	            raise RuntimeError("Host mamba reference counter is already zero.")
146	
147	    def get_last_hash_value(self) -> Optional[str]:
148	        """Returns the hash value of the last page in this node."""
149	        if self.hash_value is None or len(self.hash_value) == 0:
150	            return None
151	        return self.hash_value[-1]
152	
153	    @lru_cache(maxsize=1)
154	    def get_prefix_hash_values(self, node: "TreeNode") -> List[str]:
155	        if node is None or node.hash_value is None:
156	            return []
157	        return node.get_prefix_hash_values(node.parent) + node.hash_value
158	
159	    def __lt__(self, other: "TreeNode"):
160	        return self.last_access_time < other.last_access_time

162	
163	def get_last_access_time() -> float64:
164	    ret = TreeNode.last_access_time_counter_float
165	    TreeNode.last_access_time_counter_float += 1.0
166	    return ret
167	
168	
169	class LRUList:
170	    def __init__(self, mamba: bool = False):
171	        self.mamba = mamba
172	        if self.mamba:
173	            self.prv = "mamba_prev"
174	            self.nxt = "mamba_next"
175	            self.lock_ref = "mamba_lock_ref"
176	        else:
177	            self.prv = "prev"
178	            self.nxt = "next"
179	            self.lock_ref = "full_lock_ref"
180	        # Initialize dummy head and tail nodes
181	        self.head = TreeNode()  # Most recently used side
182	        self.tail = TreeNode()  # Least recently used side
183	        setattr(self.head, self.nxt, self.tail)  # self.head.next = self.tail
184	        setattr(self.tail, self.prv, self.head)  # self.tail.prev = self.head
185	        self.cache = {}
186	
187	    def _add_node(self, node):
188	        """Helper to add node right after head (most recently used)"""
189	        self._add_node_after(self.head, node)
190	
191	    def _add_node_after(self, old_node, new_node):
192	        """Helper to add node right after old_node"""
193	        setattr(new_node, self.prv, old_node)  # new_node.prev = old_node
194	        setattr(
195	            new_node, self.nxt, getattr(old_node, self.nxt)
196	        )  # new_node.next = old_node.next
197	        setattr(
198	            getattr(old_node, self.nxt), self.prv, new_node
199	        )  # old_node.next.prev = new_node
200	        setattr(old_node, self.nxt, new_node)  # old_node.next = new_node
201	
202	    def _remove_node(self, node):
203	        """Helper to remove node from linked list"""
204	        setattr(
205	            getattr(node, self.prv), self.nxt, getattr(node, self.nxt)
206	        )  # node.prev.next = node.next
207	        setattr(
208	            getattr(node, self.nxt), self.prv, getattr(node, self.prv)
209	        )  # node.next.prev = node.prev
210	
211	    def _get_lru(self) -> Optional[TreeNode]:
212	        """
213	        Get the least recently used node
214	        """
215	        if len(self.cache) == 0:
216	            return None
217	        return getattr(self.tail, self.prv)
218	
219	    def reset_node_mru(self, node):
220	        """
221	        Move a (existing) node to most recently used position
222	        """
223	        assert node.id in self.cache, f"Resetting node {node.id=} not in lru list"
224	        assert (
225	            not self.mamba or node.mamba_value is not None
226	        ), f"Resetting mamba tombstone node in mamba lru list: {node.id=}"
227	        self._remove_node(node)
228	        self._add_node(node)
229	
230	    def reset_node_and_parents_mru(self, node, root_node):
231	        """
232	        Move an (existing) node and its parents to most recently used position. Child node is
233	        more recently used than parent node.
234	        """
235	        prev_node = self.head
236	        while node != root_node:
237	            if not self.mamba or node.mamba_value is not None:
238	                assert (
239	                    node.id in self.cache
240	                ), f"Resetting node {node.id=} not in lru list when resetting node and parents mru"
241	                self._remove_node(node)
242	                self._add_node_after(prev_node, node)
243	                prev_node = node
244	            node = node.parent
245	
246	    def insert_mru(self, node):
247	        """
248	        Insert a (new) node as most recently used
249	        """
250	        assert (
251	            not self.mamba or node.mamba_value is not None
252	        ), f"Inserting mamba tombstone node in mamba lru list: {node.id=}"
253	        assert (
254	            node.id not in self.cache
255	        ), f"Inserting node {node.id=} already in lru list, existing node: {self.cache[node.id].id=}"
256	        self.cache[node.id] = node
257	        self._add_node(node)
258	
259	    def remove_node(self, node: TreeNode):
260	        """
261	        Remove node from lru list
262	        """
263	        assert node.id in self.cache, f"Removing node {node.id=} not in lru list"
264	        assert (
265	            not self.mamba or node.mamba_value is not None
266	        ), f"Removing mamba tombstone node from mamba lru list: {node.id=}"
267	        del self.cache[node.id]
268	        self._remove_node(node)
269	
270	    def get_lru_no_lock(self) -> Optional[TreeNode]:
271	        """
272	        Get the least recently used node that is not locked
273	        """
274	        return self.get_prev_no_lock(self.tail, check_id=False)
275	
276	    def get_leaf_lru_no_lock(self) -> Optional[TreeNode]:
277	        """
278	        Get the least recently used leaf node that is not locked
279	        """
280	        return self.get_prev_leaf_no_lock(self.tail, check_id=False)
281	
282	    def get_prev_no_lock(
283	        self, node: TreeNode, check_id: bool = True
284	    ) -> Optional[TreeNode]:
285	        """
286	        Get the previous (i.e. more recently used) node that is not locked
287	        """
288	        if check_id:
289	            assert (
290	                node.id in self.cache
291	            ), f"Getting prev of node {node.id=} not in lru list"
292	        x = getattr(node, self.prv)  # x = node.prev
293	        while getattr(x, self.lock_ref) > 0:
294	            x = getattr(x, self.prv)  # x = x.prev
295	        # if x is the head, it means there is no node in the lru list without lock
296	        if x == self.head:
297	            return None
298	        return x
299	
300	    def get_prev_leaf_no_lock(self, node: TreeNode, check_id: bool = True):
301	        """
302	        Get the previous (i.e. more recently used) leaf node that is not locked
303	        """
304	        if check_id:
305	            assert (
306	                node.id in self.cache
307	            ), f"Getting prev of node {node.id=} not in lru list"
308	        x = getattr(node, self.prv)  # x = node.prev
309	        while getattr(x, self.lock_ref) > 0 or len(x.children) > 0:
310	            x = getattr(x, self.prv)  # x = x.prev
311	        # if x is the head, it means there is no leaf node in the lru list without lock
312	        if x == self.head:
313	            return None
314	        return x
315	
316	    def in_list(self, node: Optional[TreeNode]):
317	        """
318	        Check if the node is in the lru list
319	        """
320	        if not node:
321	            return False
322	        return node.id in self.cache
323	
324	    def pretty_print(self, tree_cache: Optional["MambaRadixCache"] = None):
325	        """
326	        Pretty print the lru list
327	        """
328	        msg = f"{self.mamba=} LRU list: "
329	        x_lru = self._get_lru()
330	        while x_lru is not None and x_lru.id in self.cache:
331	            msg += f"[{x_lru.id}] {x_lru.last_access_time:f} -> "
332	            x_lru = getattr(x_lru, self.prv)
333	        print(msg)
334	
335	        if not tree_cache:
336	            return
337	        msg = f"{self.mamba=} Nodes (sorted by last_access_time): "
338	        if self.mamba:
339	            nodes = tree_cache._collect_nontombstone_nodes()
340	        else:
341	            nodes = tree_cache._collect_all_nodes()
342	        heapq.heapify(nodes)
343	        while len(nodes):
344	            x = heapq.heappop(nodes)
345	            msg += f"[{x.id}] {x.last_access_time:f} -> "
346	        print(msg)
347	
348	    # Note: this is expensive, only use for debug
349	    def sanity_check_evictable_size(self):
350	        """
351	        Check the evictable size (i.e. the size of the nodes that are not locked)
352	        """
353	        node = self.get_lru_no_lock()
354	        evictable_size = 0
355	        while self.in_list(node):
356	            evictable_size += (
357	                len(node.value) if not self.mamba else len(node.mamba_value)
358	            )
359	            node = self.get_prev_no_lock(node)
360	        return evictable_size
361	
362	    # Note: this is expensive, only use for debug or idle check
363	    def sanity_check(self, tree_cache: "MambaRadixCache"):
364	        """
365	        Check if the lru list is valid by rebuilding the lru list from the tree, heapifying it, and
366	        checking if the lru list is valid.
367	        """
368	        try:
369	            if self.mamba:
370	                nodes = tree_cache._collect_nontombstone_nodes()
371	            else:
372	                nodes = tree_cache._collect_all_nodes()
373	            total_nodes = len(nodes)
374	            total_lru = len(self.cache)
375	            # heapify based on last_access_time
376	            heapq.heapify(nodes)
377	            # the root node is not in the lru list
378	            assert len(nodes) == (
379	                total_lru + (0 if self.mamba else 1)
380	            ), f"len(nodes): {len(nodes)}, total_lru: {total_lru}"
381	
382	            x_lru = self._get_lru()
383	            while len(nodes):
384	                x = heapq.heappop(nodes)
385	                if x == tree_cache.root_node:
386	                    # root node is not in the lru list
387	                    continue
388	                assert (
389	                    x_lru is not None and x_lru.id in self.cache
390	                ), f"Incorrect LRU list, x_lru is None or not in cache: {x_lru=}, {x.id=}"
391	
392	                assert (
393	                    x == x_lru
394	                ), f"Incorrect LRU list, {self.mamba=}, x: {x.id=} != x_lru: {x_lru.id=}, {x.last_access_time=}, {x_lru.last_access_time=}"
395	                assert (
396	                    x_lru.full_lock_ref == 0
397	                ), f"x_lru should not be locked when idle, {x_lru.full_lock_ref=}, {x_lru.id=}"
398	                assert (
399	                    x_lru.mamba_lock_ref == 0
400	                ), f"x_lru should not be locked when idle, {x_lru.mamba_lock_ref=}, {x_lru.id=}"
401	                x_lru = getattr(x, self.prv)
402	
403	            if self.mamba:
404	                evictable_size = tree_cache.mamba_evictable_size()
405	                lru_list_evictable_size = self.sanity_check_evictable_size()
406	            else:
407	                evictable_size = tree_cache.full_evictable_size()
408	                lru_list_evictable_size = self.sanity_check_evictable_size()
409	
410	            assert (
411	                evictable_size == lru_list_evictable_size
412	            ), f"{self.mamba=}, total nodes: {total_nodes}, total lru: {total_lru}, evictable size: {evictable_size} != lru list evictable size: {lru_list_evictable_size}"
413	        except Exception as e:
414	            if get_tensor_model_parallel_rank() == 0:
415	                msg = f"Mamba Radix tree sanity check failed, ping @yizhang2077: {e}"

423	class MambaRadixCache(BasePrefixCache):
424	    def __init__(self, params: CacheInitParams):
425	        assert isinstance(
426	            params.token_to_kv_pool_allocator, TokenToKVPoolAllocator
427	        ) or isinstance(params.token_to_kv_pool_allocator, PagedTokenToKVPoolAllocator)
428	        self.req_to_token_pool: HybridReqToTokenPool = params.req_to_token_pool
429	        self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator = params.token_to_kv_pool_allocator
430	
431	        self.page_size = params.page_size
432	        self.disable = params.disable
433	        self.enable_mamba_extra_buffer = params.enable_mamba_extra_buffer
434	
435	        if not self.enable_mamba_extra_buffer:
436	            assert (
437	                self.page_size == 1
438	            ), f"Page size must be 1 for MambaRadixCache v1, got {self.page_size}"
439	
440	        if self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator:
441	            self.device = self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator.device
442	        else:
443	            self.device = torch.device("cpu")
444	
445	        if params.enable_metrics:
446	            self.init_metrics_collector()
447	
448	        if self.page_size == 1:
449	            self.key_match_fn = _key_match_page_size1
450	            self.get_child_key_fn = get_child_key
451	        else:
452	            self.key_match_fn = partial(_key_match_paged, page_size=self.page_size)
453	            self.get_child_key_fn = partial(get_child_key, page_size=self.page_size)
454	        self.reset()

461	    def reset(self) -> None:
462	        self.root_node = TreeNode()
463	        self.root_node.key = RadixKey([], None)
464	        self.root_node.value = []
465	        self.root_node.full_lock_ref = 1
466	        self.root_node.mamba_lock_ref = 1
467	        self.full_evictable_size_ = 0
468	        self.mamba_evictable_size_ = 0
469	        self.full_protected_size_ = 0
470	        self.mamba_protected_size_ = 0
471	        # LRU lists are used to maintain the order of eviction of the nodes in the tree
472	        self.full_lru_list = LRUList(mamba=False)
473	        self.mamba_lru_list = LRUList(mamba=True)

475	    def match_prefix(self, params: MatchPrefixParams) -> MatchResult:
476	        """Find the matching prefix from the radix tree.
477	        Args:
478	            params: MatchPrefixParams containing key and optional Mamba-specific parameters.
479	        Returns:
480	            A tuple of a tensor of matching prefix token IDs and
481	            the last node that contains the prefix values. Note that
482	            this API can modify the internal state of the Radix tree.
483	            The last node create a new child if the prefix is shorter
484	            than the last node's value.
485	        """
486	        key = self._match_pre_processor(params)
487	        if key is None:
488	            return MatchResult(
489	                device_indices=torch.empty(
490	                    (0,),
491	                    dtype=torch.int64,
492	                    device=self.device,
493	                ),
494	                last_device_node=self.root_node,
495	                last_host_node=self.root_node,
496	            )
497	
498	        value, last_node, best_value_len = self._match_prefix_helper(key)
499	        return self._match_post_processor(params, value, last_node, best_value_len)
500	
501	    def insert(self, params: InsertParams) -> InsertResult:
502	        if self.disable:
503	            return InsertResult(prefix_len=0, mamba_exist=False)
504	
505	        key = params.key
506	        value = params.value
507	        mamba_value = params.mamba_value
508	        prev_prefix_len = params.prev_prefix_len
509	
510	        if value is None:
511	            value = torch.tensor([x for x in key.token_ids], dtype=torch.int64)
512	        prefix_len, mamba_exist = self._insert_helper(
513	            self.root_node, key, value, mamba_value, params.chunked, prev_prefix_len
514	        )
515	        return InsertResult(prefix_len=prefix_len, mamba_exist=mamba_exist)

517	    def cache_finished_req(self, req: Req, is_insert: bool = True) -> None:
518	        """Cache request when it finishes."""
519	        kv_committed_len = req.pop_committed_kv_cache()
520	        if self.disable:
521	            kv_indices = self.req_to_token_pool.req_to_token[
522	                req.req_pool_idx, :kv_committed_len
523	            ]
524	            self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator.free(kv_indices)
525	            self.req_to_token_pool.free_mamba_cache(req)
526	            return
527	
528	        token_ids = (req.origin_input_ids + req.output_ids)[:kv_committed_len]
529	        kv_indices = self.req_to_token_pool.req_to_token[
530	            req.req_pool_idx, :kv_committed_len
531	        ]
532	
533	        if is_insert:
534	            cache_len = (
535	                req.mamba_last_track_seqlen
536	                if self.enable_mamba_extra_buffer
537	                else len(token_ids)
538	            )
539	            if cache_len is None:
540	                cache_len = 0
541	            if cache_len != len(token_ids):
542	                cache_end_idx = max(cache_len, req.cache_protected_len)
543	                self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator.free(kv_indices[cache_end_idx:])
544	                token_ids = token_ids[:cache_len]
545	                kv_indices = kv_indices[:cache_len]
546	
547	            if self.page_size != 1:
548	                page_aligned_len = len(kv_indices) // self.page_size * self.page_size
549	                page_aligned_kv_indices = kv_indices[:page_aligned_len].to(
550	                    dtype=torch.int64, copy=True
551	                )
552	            else:
553	                page_aligned_len = len(kv_indices)
554	                page_aligned_kv_indices = kv_indices.to(dtype=torch.int64, copy=True)
555	
556	            assert (
557	                cache_len == page_aligned_len
558	            ), f"It is required {cache_len=}, {page_aligned_len=}, {kv_committed_len=}, {len(req.origin_input_ids)=}, {len(req.output_ids)=} ping @yizhang2077 if you see this"
559	
560	            # Radix Cache takes one ref in memory pool
561	            # insert the token_ids and kv_indices into the radix tree
562	            if self.enable_mamba_extra_buffer:
563	                mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer_to_keep = (
564	                    self.req_to_token_pool.get_mamba_ping_pong_other_idx(
565	                        req.mamba_next_track_idx
566	                    )
567	                )
568	                mamba_value = (
569	                    req.mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer[
570	                        mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer_to_keep
571	                    ]
572	                    .unsqueeze(-1)
573	                    .clone()
574	                )
575	            else:
576	                mamba_value = req.mamba_pool_idx.unsqueeze(-1).clone()
577	                mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer_to_keep = None
578	
579	            result = self.insert(
580	                InsertParams(
581	                    key=RadixKey(token_ids[:page_aligned_len], req.extra_key),
582	                    value=page_aligned_kv_indices,
583	                    mamba_value=mamba_value,
584	                    prev_prefix_len=req.cache_protected_len,
585	                )
586	            )
587	            mamba_exist = result.mamba_exist
588	        else:
589	            self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator.free(kv_indices[req.cache_protected_len :])
590	            mamba_exist = True
591	
592	        if mamba_exist:
593	            mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer_to_keep = None
594	
595	        free_mamba_cache = True if self.enable_mamba_extra_buffer else mamba_exist
596	
597	        if free_mamba_cache:
598	            self.req_to_token_pool.free_mamba_cache(
599	                req,
600	                mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer_to_keep=mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer_to_keep,
601	            )
602	
603	        self.dec_lock_ref(req.last_node)
604	
605	    def cache_unfinished_req(self, req: Req, chunked=False) -> None:
606	        """Cache request when it is unfinished."""
607	
608	        def _skip_cache_unfinished_req(req: Req) -> None:
609	            kv_indices = self.req_to_token_pool.req_to_token[
610	                req.req_pool_idx, : len(req.fill_ids)
611	            ]
612	
613	            # `req.prefix_indices` will be used in `PrefillAdder::add_chunked_req` later
614	            req.prefix_indices = kv_indices.to(dtype=torch.int64, copy=True)
615	            return
616	
617	        token_ids = req.fill_ids
618	        cache_len = (
619	            req.mamba_last_track_seqlen
620	            if self.enable_mamba_extra_buffer
621	            else len(token_ids)
622	        )
623	        if self.disable or cache_len is None:
624	            return _skip_cache_unfinished_req(req)
625	
626	        kv_indices_orig = self.req_to_token_pool.req_to_token[
627	            req.req_pool_idx, : len(token_ids)
628	        ]
629	        # kv_indices is the kv indices to be cached
630	        kv_indices = kv_indices_orig[:cache_len]
631	        if self.page_size != 1:
632	            page_aligned_len = len(kv_indices) // self.page_size * self.page_size
633	            page_aligned_kv_indices = kv_indices[:page_aligned_len].to(
634	                dtype=torch.int64, copy=True
635	            )
636	        else:
637	            page_aligned_len = len(kv_indices)
638	            page_aligned_kv_indices = kv_indices.to(dtype=torch.int64, copy=True)
639	
640	        assert page_aligned_len == len(
641	            kv_indices
642	        ), f"page_aligned_len != len(kv_indices), {page_aligned_len=}, {len(kv_indices)=}, {cache_len=}, {self.page_size=}, {FLA_CHUNK_SIZE=}"
643	
644	        page_aligned_token_ids = token_ids[:page_aligned_len]
645	
646	        if self.enable_mamba_extra_buffer:
647	            # copy from the ping pong track buffer
648	            mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer_to_keep = (
649	                self.req_to_token_pool.get_mamba_ping_pong_other_idx(
650	                    req.mamba_next_track_idx
651	                )
652	            )
653	            mamba_value = (
654	                req.mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer[mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer_to_keep]
655	                .unsqueeze(-1)
656	                .clone()
657	            )
658	        else:
659	            mamba_value = self.req_to_token_pool.get_mamba_indices(
660	                req.req_pool_idx
661	            ).unsqueeze(-1)
662	        # radix tree mamba value is forked from req space
663	        mamba_value_forked = self.req_to_token_pool.mamba_pool.fork_from(mamba_value)
664	
665	        # if alloc mamba cache failed, do evict and alloc again
666	        if mamba_value_forked is None:
667	            self.evict(EvictParams(num_tokens=0, mamba_num=1))
668	            mamba_value_forked = self.req_to_token_pool.mamba_pool.fork_from(
669	                mamba_value
670	            )
671	            assert mamba_value_forked is not None, "Can not alloc mamba cache"
672	        result = self.insert(
673	            InsertParams(
674	                key=RadixKey(page_aligned_token_ids, req.extra_key),
675	                value=page_aligned_kv_indices,
676	                mamba_value=mamba_value_forked,
677	                prev_prefix_len=req.cache_protected_len,
678	            )
679	        )
680	        new_prefix_len, mamba_exist = result.prefix_len, result.mamba_exist
681	        # there is a mamba cache in radix cache, release it
682	        if mamba_exist:
683	            self.req_to_token_pool.mamba_pool.free(mamba_value_forked)
684	
685	        # The prefix indices could be updated, reuse it
686	        match_result = self.match_prefix(
687	            MatchPrefixParams(key=RadixKey(page_aligned_token_ids, req.extra_key))
688	        )
689	        new_indices, new_last_node = (
690	            match_result.device_indices,
691	            match_result.last_device_node,
692	        )
693	
694	        if not mamba_exist:
695	            assert torch.equal(new_last_node.mamba_value, mamba_value_forked)
696	
697	        assert (
698	            req.cache_protected_len <= len(new_indices) + self.page_size - 1
699	        ), f"{req.cache_protected_len=}, {len(new_indices)=}, {len(page_aligned_token_ids)=}, {mamba_exist=}"
700	        assert new_prefix_len <= len(
701	            new_indices
702	        ), f"{new_prefix_len=}, {len(new_indices)=}"
703	
704	        self.req_to_token_pool.write(
705	            (req.req_pool_idx, slice(req.cache_protected_len, len(new_indices))),
706	            new_indices[req.cache_protected_len :],
707	        )
708	
709	        self.dec_lock_ref(req.last_node)
710	        self.inc_lock_ref(new_last_node)
711	
712	        # `req.prefix_indices` will be used in `PrefillAdder::add_chunked_req` later
713	        # NOTE: this is needed for both page_size == 1 and page_size > 1
714	        req.prefix_indices = torch.cat(
715	            [new_indices, kv_indices_orig[len(new_indices) :]]
716	        )
717	        req.cache_protected_len = len(new_indices)
718	        req.mamba_last_track_seqlen = None
719	        req.last_node = new_last_node
720

729	    def _evict_leaf_node(
730	        self, x: TreeNode, is_evict_mamba: bool
731	    ) -> Tuple[int, int, TreeNode, TreeNode]:
732	        assert (
733	            x.full_lock_ref == 0 and x.mamba_lock_ref == 0
734	        ), f"evict leaf node invalid with {x.id=} {x.full_lock_ref=} {x.mamba_lock_ref=}"
735	
736	        assert x.mamba_value is not None, f"leaf node mamba value is not None, {x.id=}"
737	        # 1. a leaf node, free full tokens and mamba
738	        self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator.free(x.value)
739	        full_num_evicted = len(x.value)
740	        self.req_to_token_pool.mamba_pool.free(x.mamba_value)
741	        mamba_num_evicted = len(x.mamba_value)
742	
743	        # 2. get the next node, update the lru lists
744	        if is_evict_mamba:
745	            x_next = self.mamba_lru_list.get_prev_no_lock(x)
746	        else:
747	            x_next = self.full_lru_list.get_prev_leaf_no_lock(x)
748	        self.full_lru_list.remove_node(x)
749	        self.mamba_lru_list.remove_node(x)
750	
751	        # 3. delete the leaf node
752	        self._delete_leaf(x)
753	
754	        # 4. Iteratively delete tombstone leaves to maintain invariant that leaf nodes are not tombstone
755	        x, leaf_full_num_evicted = self._iteratively_delete_tombstone_leaf(x)
756	        full_num_evicted += leaf_full_num_evicted
757	        return full_num_evicted, mamba_num_evicted, x, x_next
758	
759	    def evict(self, params: EvictParams) -> EvictResult:
760	        if self.disable:
761	            return EvictResult()
762	
763	        full_num_evicted = 0
764	        mamba_num_evicted = 0
765	
766	        if params.num_tokens > 0:
767	            full_num_evicted = self.evict_full(params.num_tokens)
768	        if params.mamba_num > 0:
769	            mamba_num_evicted = self.evict_mamba(params.mamba_num)
770	
771	        return EvictResult(
772	            num_tokens_evicted=full_num_evicted, mamba_num_evicted=mamba_num_evicted
773	        )
774	
775	    def evict_mamba(self, mamba_num: int) -> int:
776	        """Evict mamba states. Returns the number of mamba states evicted."""
777	        if self.disable or mamba_num <= 0:
778	            return 0
779	        # get the least recently used node that is not locked, doesn't have to be a leaf
780	        x = self.mamba_lru_list.get_lru_no_lock()
781	        mamba_num_evicted = 0
782	        # evict lru leaf nodes until mamba_num_tokens is reached
783	        while mamba_num_evicted < mamba_num and (self.mamba_lru_list.in_list(x)):
784	            assert x.mamba_value is not None, f"node has no mamba value, {x.id=}"
785	            assert (
786	                len(x.mamba_value) == 1
787	            ), f"node has abnormal mamba length, {x.id=}, {len(x.mamba_value)=}"
788	            assert x != self.root_node, f"root node is not evictable, {x.id=}"
789	            assert x.mamba_lock_ref == 0, f"node is in use by mamba kv indices, {x.id=}"
790	
791	            if len(x.children) > 0:
792	                # 1. an internal node, free mamba tokens.
793	                self.req_to_token_pool.mamba_pool.free(x.mamba_value)
794	                mamba_num_evicted += len(x.mamba_value)
795	
796	                # 2. get the next node, update the lru lists
797	                x_next = self.mamba_lru_list.get_prev_no_lock(x)
798	                self.mamba_lru_list.remove_node(x)
799	
800	                # 3. tombstone the node
801	                self._tombstone_internal_node(x)
802	            else:
803	                _, mamba_evicted_delta, _, x_next = self._evict_leaf_node(x, True)
804	                mamba_num_evicted += mamba_evicted_delta
805	
806	            x = x_next
807	
808	        return mamba_num_evicted
809	
810	    def evict_full(self, full_num_tokens: int) -> int:
811	        """Evict full KV cache. Returns the number of tokens evicted."""
812	        if self.disable or full_num_tokens <= 0:
813	            return 0
814	
815	        full_num_evicted = 0
816	        # get the least recently used leaf node that is not locked
817	        x = self.full_lru_list.get_leaf_lru_no_lock()
818	
819	        while full_num_evicted < full_num_tokens and self.full_lru_list.in_list(x):
820	            assert (
821	                x != self.root_node
822	            ), f"root node should not exist in full lru list, {x.id=}"
823	            full_num_evicted_delta, _, x, x_next = self._evict_leaf_node(x, False)
824	            full_num_evicted += full_num_evicted_delta
825	
826	            # if parent has no more children, it is a leaf. It is possible that this node is lru, so
827	            # we need to get the first leaf node in the lru list
828	            if len(x.parent.children) == 0:
829	                x_next = self.full_lru_list.get_leaf_lru_no_lock()
830	
831	            x = x_next
832	
833	        return full_num_evicted

835	    def inc_lock_ref(self, node: TreeNode) -> IncLockRefResult:
836	        """
837	        Increment the lock reference count for the node.
838	        It locks the full_lock_ref for nodes between the [last node, root), exclusive.
839	        It locks the mamba_lock_ref for current node if its mamba_value exists.
840	        """
841	        if self.disable:
842	            return IncLockRefResult()
843	
844	        # protect mamba value in current node if it exists
845	        if node.mamba_value is not None:
846	            if node.mamba_lock_ref == 0:
847	                self.mamba_evictable_size_ -= len(node.mamba_value)
848	                self.mamba_protected_size_ += len(node.mamba_value)
849	            node.mamba_lock_ref += 1
850	
851	        while node != self.root_node:
852	            # lock full from node to root
853	            assert (
854	                node.full_lock_ref >= 0
855	            ), f"inc_lock_ref on node with {node.full_lock_ref=}, {node.id=}"
856	            if node.full_lock_ref == 0:
857	                self.full_evictable_size_ -= len(node.value)
858	                self.full_protected_size_ += len(node.value)
859	            node.full_lock_ref += 1
860	            node = node.parent
861	        return IncLockRefResult()
862	
863	    def dec_lock_ref(
864	        self, node: TreeNode, params: Optional[DecLockRefParams] = None
865	    ) -> DecLockRefResult:
866	        """
867	        Decrement the lock reference count for the node.
868	        It unlocks the full_lock_ref for nodes between the [last node, root), exclusive.
869	        It unlocks the mamba_lock_ref for current node if its mamba_value exists.
870	        """
871	        if self.disable:
872	            return DecLockRefResult()
873	
874	        if node.mamba_value is not None:
875	            assert (
876	                node.mamba_lock_ref > 0
877	            ), f"dec_lock_ref on node with {node.mamba_lock_ref=}, {node.id=}"
878	            if node.mamba_lock_ref == 1:
879	                self.mamba_evictable_size_ += len(node.mamba_value)
880	                self.mamba_protected_size_ -= len(node.mamba_value)
881	            node.mamba_lock_ref -= 1
882	
883	        while node != self.root_node:
884	            assert (
885	                node.full_lock_ref > 0
886	            ), f"dec_lock_ref on node with {node.full_lock_ref=}, {node.id=}"
887	            if node.full_lock_ref == 1:
888	                self.full_evictable_size_ += len(node.value)
889	                self.full_protected_size_ -= len(node.value)
890	            node.full_lock_ref -= 1
891	            node = node.parent
892	
893	        return DecLockRefResult()

954	    ##### Internal Helper Functions #####
955	
956	    def _match_prefix_helper(
957	        self, key: RadixKey
958	    ) -> Tuple[List[torch.Tensor], TreeNode, int]:
959	        """
960	        Mamba prefix matching helper. It factors in the sliding window size such that
961	        the matched node is guaranteed to either 1. connected to root without mamba tombstone,
962	        or 2. the number of matching tokens from the matched node to the last mamba tombstone
963	        node is greater than or equal to the sliding window size.
964	        """
965	        node = self.root_node
966	        child_key = self.get_child_key_fn(key)
967	
968	        value: List[torch.Tensor] = []

## 2. How `extra_buffer` (specifically `enable_mamba_extra_buffer`) Is Used

The flag `self.enable_mamba_extra_buffer` is set from `params.enable_mamba_extra_buffer` at **line 433** (defaults to `False` per `CacheInitParams` at `cache_init_params.py:28`).

### Constraint when disabled (line 435-438):
When `enable_mamba_extra_buffer` is `False`, page_size must be 1. This is the "v1" mode.

### Effect on `cache_finished_req` (lines 533-601):

**Determining `cache_len`** (lines 535-538): When enabled, `cache_len = req.mamba_last_track_seqlen` (the sequence length at the last mamba state checkpoint). When disabled, `cache_len = len(token_ids)` (full sequence).

**Constructing `mamba_value`** (lines 562-577): When enabled, the mamba value is taken from `req.mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer` -- it reads from the "other" ping-pong buffer (the one NOT being written to next, i.e., the one that has the last committed state), unsqueezes and clones. When disabled, the mamba value comes directly from `req.mamba_pool_idx`.

**Free logic** (line 595): When enabled, `free_mamba_cache` is always `True` (the request's mamba cache is always freed since the state was captured into the radix tree). When disabled, free only happens if `mamba_exist` is `True` (i.e., the tree already had a mamba state at that node).

**Passing `mamba_ping_pong_track_buffer_to_keep`** (lines 598-601): When enabled, this buffer index is passed to `free_mamba_cache` so the pool can selectively keep one ping-pong buffer. When disabled, it is `None`.

### Effect on `cache_unfinished_req` (lines 618-657):

Same pattern: `cache_len` is `req.mamba_last_track_seqlen` (enabled) vs `len(token_ids)` (disabled), and `mamba_value` is sourced from the ping-pong track buffer (enabled) vs direct mamba pool indices (disabled).

### Summary of extra_buffer purpose:
The "extra buffer" mechanism implements a **ping-pong double-buffering** scheme for mamba states. During forward passes, the model writes mamba states into alternating buffers. When caching a request (finished or unfinished), the system reads from the buffer that was last fully written (the "other" one relative to `mamba_next_track_idx`). This allows mamba state snapshots to be taken at chunk boundaries (`mamba_last_track_seqlen`) rather than requiring the full sequence to be processed, enabling chunked prefill for Mamba/hybrid models. When disabled (v1 mode), there is a single mamba pool index per request and the entire sequence must be processed.

## 3. How Mamba State Tracking Interacts with Prefix Caching

### Radix tree node structure (lines 66-107):
Each `TreeNode` has:
- `value`: KV cache indices (full attention)
- `mamba_value`: Mamba state pool indices
- `mamba_host_value`: Mamba state backed up to host
- Separate lock reference counts: `full_lock_ref` and `mamba_lock_ref`

### "Tombstone" concept:
A **mamba tombstone** is a tree node where `mamba_value is None` but the node still exists with KV cache data (`value`). This arises because mamba states can be evicted independently from full KV cache. The property `mamba_evicted` (line 114-115) checks this.

### Dual LRU lists (lines 462-473):
The cache maintains **two separate LRU lists**: `full_lru_list` for KV cache eviction order, and `mamba_lru_list` for mamba state eviction order. The mamba LRU list only contains nodes with non-None `mamba_value` (non-tombstoned nodes).

### Lock reference invariant (lines 79-83):
- If `mamba_lock_ref > 0`, then `full_lock_ref > 0` (mamba lock implies full lock)
- If `full_lock_ref > 0`, `mamba_lock_ref` can be 0 (full lock does NOT imply mamba lock)
- Full lock propagates from node to root; mamba lock only applies to the node itself

### Prefix matching with mamba awareness (lines 956-996, `_match_prefix_helper`):
The prefix matching algorithm tracks `best_value_len` and `best_last_node`. It only updates these when it encounters a node with `mamba_value is not None`. This means the effective prefix match stops at the **deepest non-tombstoned node**. If intermediate nodes are tombstoned (mamba evicted), the prefix match truncates to the last node that has a mamba state, because the mamba state is needed to continue computation.

### Post-processing with mamba branching (lines 1007-1079, `_match_post_processor`):
When the actual matched value extends beyond `best_value_len` (meaning there are tombstoned nodes deeper in the tree), the system computes `mamba_branching_seqlen` -- a chunk-aligned sequence length indicating where the mamba state fork should happen. This tells the model runner that tokens beyond this point need re-computation of mamba states even though KV cache exists.

### Copy-on-write mamba states (lines 1049-1066):
During prefix matching with `cow_mamba=True`, the matched node's mamba state is **copied** into the request's private mamba pool. This is copy-on-write: the tree's mamba state is not modified; instead, a fresh copy is allocated for the request to use during its forward pass. If allocation fails, the system evicts mamba states (while protecting the matched node) and retries.

### Eviction: independent mamba eviction (lines 775-808, `evict_mamba`):
Mamba states can be evicted independently from KV cache. When an **internal node** (has children) is mamba-evicted, it becomes a tombstone -- its `mamba_value` is set to `None`, it's removed from the mamba LRU list, but its KV cache data (`value`) and position in the full LRU list remain. When a **leaf node** is mamba-evicted, both its KV cache and mamba state are freed, and the node is deleted (since a leaf without mamba state is useless).

### Insert with mamba state (lines 1111-1181, `_insert_helper`):
On insertion, if the key fully matches an existing node that has been tombstoned (`node.mamba_value is None`), the mamba value is restored (line 1169-1174). If the node already has a mamba value, the insertion reports `mamba_value_exist = True` (line 1176). For new nodes, both `value` and `mamba_value` are set.

### Tombstone cleanup (lines 1183-1204, `_iteratively_delete_tombstone_leaf`):
After evicting a leaf node, the system checks if the parent became a tombstone leaf (no children, no mamba value). If so, it iteratively cleans up the parent chain, freeing KV cache and removing from the full LRU list, up to the root or a locked node.

## 4. Host-Device Synchronization That Would Stall the GPU Pipeline

### Direct synchronization calls:
There are **no explicit** `torch.cuda.synchronize()`, `.item()`, `.cpu()`, or `.tolist()` calls in this file.

### Implicit synchronization points:

**`torch.equal()` at line 695:**
```python
            assert torch.equal(new_last_node.mamba_value, mamba_value_forked)
```
`torch.equal()` returns a Python `bool`, which requires reading a GPU result to the host. This is an implicit synchronization point. However, it is inside an `assert` statement in `cache_unfinished_req`, so it would only execute in debug mode (when assertions are enabled).

**`len()` on GPU tensors** throughout the file:
Calls like `len(node.value)`, `len(x.value)`, `len(x.mamba_value)`, `len(kv_indices)`, etc., appear extensively (lines 357, 534, 539, 541, 547-548, 553, 631-632, 637, 698-702, 717, 739, 741, 787, 794, 857, 879, 889, 1090, 1100, 1162, 1167-1168, 1199, 1215-1216, 1220, 1232, 1283-1286). `len()` on a PyTorch tensor returns `tensor.shape[0]`, which is metadata and does **NOT** trigger a device-to-host transfer -- this is safe.

**`.clone()` calls** at lines 573, 576, 656, 1090, 1100, 1162:
`.clone()` on GPU tensors stays on GPU and does not synchronize. These are safe.

**`.to(dtype=torch.int64, copy=True)`** at lines 549-550, 554, 614, 633-634, 638:
These are dtype casts on the same device. They do NOT trigger host-device synchronization.

**`torch.cat()`** at lines 714, 932, 944, 1070:
These operate on GPU tensors and stay on GPU. No synchronization.

**`torch.tensor([x for x in key.token_ids], ...)`** at line 511:
```python
            value = torch.tensor([x for x in key.token_ids], dtype=torch.int64)
```
This creates a tensor from a Python list on CPU (default device). This tensor is used as `value` but is later cloned into tree nodes. Since `key.token_ids` is already a Python-side list, this doesn't involve GPU synchronization. However, note that this creates a **CPU tensor** while other values in the tree might be on GPU -- this could be a latent issue depending on how `value` is consumed downstream.

### Summary of sync risks:
The file is largely clean of GPU-stalling operations. The only real implicit sync is the `torch.equal()` assertion at line 695, which would stall the pipeline when assertions are enabled. All other operations either stay on-device or work with metadata/Python-side data.
